steps.title: Identify what the question needs
steps.content: The question asks for the function of the underlined sentence in the text as a whole. The underlined sentence describes Dot exercising her legs in her shopping-cart stroller. It follows the general claim "Day by day, Dot grew stronger."

steps.title: Eliminate the choices
steps.content:
• Choice A — ‘It makes a prediction about how Celestine will change as she grows older.’: The sentence says nothing about Celestine's future — it describes Dot's current behavior. ✗
  • Choice B — ‘It explores some of the narrator's psychological characteristics.’: The sentence is about Dot's physical activity, not the narrator's psychology. ✗
  • Choice C — ‘It offers a detail that shows how Dot is growing stronger.’: Dot bouncing in the stroller is a concrete example of her physical development, directly supporting the prior claim that she grew stronger. ✓
  • Choice D — ‘It indicates how sad Celestine is by describing a behavior she engages in.’: Celestine does not appear in the underlined sentence; Dot is the one acting. ✗
That confirms C is correct.
